text_block_list.name: Taiki Shuttle
text_block_list.text:
Howdy, Flower! Be a pal and tell me about a
Japanese sayin' I heard!

text_block_list.name: Taiki Shuttle
text_block_list.text:
It went somethin' like, "It's dark without a light."
Is that right? I heard Grass say it not a minute
ago.

text_block_list.name: Nishino Flower
text_block_list.text:
Hmm, it was probably... "It's darkest beneath the
lamp," right?

text_block_list.name: Nishino Flower
text_block_list.text:
It's a metaphor for how easy it is to lose sight of
things when they're right beside you. Even lamps
cast a shadow beneath themselves, you see.

text_block_list.name: Taiki Shuttle
text_block_list.text: Oh, the shadow! I know another sayin' like that!

text_block_list.name: Taiki Shuttle
text_block_list.text:
"Take shelter in the shade of a taiki"! But what's
that word mean?

text_block_list.name: Nishino Flower
text_block_list.text:
That proverb is about how you should rely on
someone big and strong when you need help. I
think you might have it a little wrong, though.

text_block_list.name: Nishino Flower
text_block_list.text:
That word means "big tree," but it's actually
pronounced "taiju."

text_block_list.name: Taiki Shuttle
text_block_list.text: You don't say... I messed that one up good.

text_block_list.name: Nishino Flower
text_block_list.text:
Hehe, well, you may have gotten the
pronunciation a little mixed up...

text_block_list.name: Nishino Flower
text_block_list.text:
But in a way, your version sounds just right to me.
After all, when I need help, that's exactly what I
think: "I should turn to Taiki!"
